answer: A user would receive a 413 'request_too_large' error when using the Claude API if they exceed the maximum request size limits, which are 32 MB for Messages, Token Counting, Sessions, Agents, and Environments, 256 MB for Batch API, and 500 MB for Files API. These limits differ from third-party platforms, where Vertex AI limits requests to 30 MB and Amazon Bedrock limits requests to 20 MB.

The Message Batches API offers a 50% cost reduction compared to the standard Messages API, but it processes messages asynchronously, which may be a trade-off for users who require real-time conversational interactions.

Direct access to the latest models and features, Anthropic billing and support, and best for new integrations, full feature access, and direct relationship with Anthropic, whereas third-party platforms may have feature delays or differences, are integrated with cloud provider billing and IAM, and are best for existing cloud commitments, specific compliance requirements, and consolidated cloud billing.

The Messages, Token Counting, Sessions, Agents, and Environments APIs have the smallest maximum request size limit of 32 MB. The largest request size limit is 500 MB for the Files API. The numerical difference between these limits is 468 MB.
